story.iWant: permission constant'larını code'dan database'e sync edecek bir script
story.soThat: development environment'ta kod değişikliklerini database'e manuel olarak girmek zorunda kalmayayım
story.tasks:
- Task 1: Create permission sync script (AC: 3.7.1) - Subtask 1.1: Create `scripts/permission-sync.ts` file - Subtask 1.2: Import PrismaService and PERMISSIONS constant - Subtask 1.3: Implement permission extraction logic (iterate PERMISSIONS object) - Subtask 1.4: For each permission, implement Prisma upsert with unique constraint [module, action] - Subtask 1.5: Handle upsert results (track new vs existing) - Subtask 1.6: Verify idempotency (can run multiple times safely)
- Task 2: Add environment protection (AC: 3.7.2) - Subtask 2.1: Check process.env.NODE_ENV at script start - Subtask 2.2: Allow only 'development' or 'local' environments - Subtask 2.3: Throw descriptive error if production/staging detected - Subtask 2.4: Log warning message before sync execution
- Task 3: Implement console logging (AC: 3.7.3) - Subtask 3.1: Count new and existing permissions during sync - Subtask 3.2: Log each newly created permission with module and action - Subtask 3.3: Log summary: "Synced X permissions: Y new, Z existing" - Subtask 3.4: Log success message on completion
- Task 4: Add npm script and documentation (AC: 3.7.4) - Subtask 4.1: Add "permission:sync" script to package.json - Subtask 4.2: Script command: "ts-node scripts/permission-sync.ts" - Subtask 4.3: Verify script executes via `npm run permission:sync` - Subtask 4.4: Add script comment in package.json: "// Dev only - syncs code permissions to DB"
- Task 5: Testing (AC: 3.7.1, 3.7.2) - Subtask 5.1: Test script with empty database (all permissions should be created) - Subtask 5.2: Test idempotency (run twice, second run should find all existing) - Subtask 5.3: Test environment check (should fail if NODE_ENV=production) - Subtask 5.4: Test partial sync (add new permission to PERMISSIONS constant, run script, verify only new one created) - Subtask 5.5: Verify console output format and accuracy

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
1. **AC-3.7.1:** scripts/permission-sync.ts created - PERMISSIONS constant'ını code'dan okur - Tüm module ve action'ları iterate eder - Her permission için upsert yapar (yeni ise insert, varsa skip) - Prisma upsert kullanır ([module, action] unique constraint ile) - Idempotent (birden fazla kez güvenle çalıştırılabilir)
2. **AC-3.7.2:** Environment check enforced - NODE_ENV kontrolü: Sadece 'development' veya 'local'da çalışır - 'production' veya 'staging'de çalıştırılırsa error throw eder - Sync öncesi warning loglar
3. **AC-3.7.3:** Console output - Log: "Synced X permissions: Y new, Z existing" - Eklenen her yeni permission loglanır - Success message ile tamamlanır
4. **AC-3.7.4:** package.json script added - Script: "permission:sync": "ts-node scripts/permission-sync.ts" - Çalıştırılabilir: npm run permission:sync

docs.doc: Complete AC specifications (AC-3.7.1 through AC-3.7.4), Permission Sync Script Flow diagram, environment protection requirements
Prisma ORM v6.16.0 usage, TypeScript Strict Mode, module organization patterns
PERMISSIONS constant created with MODULE.ACTION format, Permission entity with unique constraint on [module, action]
Permission table exists with unique constraint [module, action], ready for upsert operations
PERMISSIONS constant location and format confirmed, testing infrastructure established, ts-node already in devDependencies

artifact.reason: Source constant that needs to be synced to database - contains all permission modules and actions

code.artifact: export const PERMISSIONS = { USERS: { CREATE, VIEW, UPDATE, DELETE }, PERMISSIONS: { VIEW, ASSIGN, REVOKE }, FILES: { CREATE, VIEW, DELETE, VIEW_ALL } }

artifact.reason: Reference for how to instantiate and use PrismaClient in standalone scripts

code.artifact: PrismaService extends PrismaClient, implements OnModuleInit/OnModuleDestroy lifecycle hooks

artifact.reason: Example script pattern - shows error handling, console output, and ts-node execution pattern

code.artifact: Reference script demonstrating: error handling with try/catch, console logging with emoji indicators, process.exit patterns, NODE_ENV checking pattern

artifact.reason: Target database table with unique constraint [module, action] for upsert operations

code.artifact: model Permission { module String, action String, @@unique([module, action]), @@index([module]) }

artifact.reason: Location to add permission:sync script, shows existing script patterns like prisma:seed

code.artifact: Existing scripts use ts-node for TypeScript execution without build step

package.name: ts-node
package.reason: Execute TypeScript scripts without build step

constraints.constraint: Script MUST check NODE_ENV and only run in 'development' or 'local' environments - throw descriptive error for production/staging
Script MUST be idempotent - running multiple times should not create duplicate permissions (use Prisma upsert)
Script MUST use Prisma unique constraint [module, action] for upsert where clause
Script MUST instantiate PrismaClient directly (no NestJS dependency injection) - standalone script pattern
Script MUST provide clear console output with counts (X total synced, Y new, Z existing) and success/error messages
Script MUST use ts-node execution (no build step required) - add to package.json scripts section
Script MUST iterate all modules in PERMISSIONS constant and extract module/action pairs
Script MUST gracefully handle database connection failures with clear error messages
Script MUST log warning message before starting sync operation
Script MUST log each newly created permission (module and action)

interface.name: PERMISSIONS constant
interface.description: Source of truth for permission definitions - iterate modules and actions to extract permission pairs

interface.name: Prisma.permission.upsert()
interface.signature: upsert({ where: { module_action: { module, action } }, update: {}, create: { module, action, description } })
interface.description: Upsert method with composite unique key - insert if new, skip if exists (idempotent)

interface.name: PrismaClient
interface.description: Direct Prisma client instantiation for standalone scripts (no DI needed)

interface.name: process.env.NODE_ENV
interface.description: Environment check - must be 'development' or 'local', error if 'production' or 'staging'

tests.standards:
Integration Testing (Script Execution): - Test with empty database (all permissions should be created) - Test idempotency (run twice, second run should find all existing) - Test environment check (should fail if NODE_ENV=production) - Test partial sync (add new permission to PERMISSIONS constant, run script, verify only new one created) - Verify console output format and counts accuracy
Manual Testing Checklist: - Run `npm run permission:sync` in fresh database - Verify all PERMISSIONS constant entries in DB - Run again, verify "0 new, X existing" output - Test NODE_ENV=production → Script fails with clear error - Add new permission to constant, sync, verify only new one added
Testing Tools: Manual script execution, database inspection, console output verification Coverage Target: Integration tests cover all AC requirements (AC-3.7.1 through AC-3.7.4)

locations.location: Manual test: Run script and verify database state via Prisma Studio or SQL queries

ideas.idea: Test: Run script with empty Permission table → Verify all PERMISSIONS entries created in DB
Test: Run script twice → Second run should log "0 new, X existing" (idempotency)
Test: Add new permission to PERMISSIONS constant → Run script → Verify only new one created
Test: Set NODE_ENV=production → Run script → Should throw error with descriptive message
Test: Set NODE_ENV=development → Run script → Should execute successfully
Test: Verify console output format matches "Synced X permissions: Y new, Z existing"
Test: Verify each new permission logged with module and action
Test: Run `npm run permission:sync` → Verify script executes via npm command
